content:
MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
NEUTRAL regime — normal volatility with very narrow participation. Vol: 25.2%. Breadth: 0/8 sectors positive, signaling pervasive weakness despite normal vol.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Heavy Industry and Energy & Chemicals are the least weak; Heavy Industry shows relative resilience led by ASELS and EREGL. No sector is positive on a 1W basis but Heavy Industry is ABOVE TREND on key names: ASELS (1W: +9.50% | 1M: +12.12%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: +0.06% | 1M: +11.78%) ABOVE TREND.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Technology & Telecom, Holding & Investment and Transportation are the weakest on 1W performance and breadth. Examples: KONTR (1W: -12.59% | 1M: -10.53%) BELOW TREND; SAHOL (1W: -9.64% | 1M: -12.28%) BELOW TREND; PGSUS (1W: -8.95% | 1M: -9.04%) BELOW TREND.

STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
ASELS (1W: +9.50% | 1M: +12.12%) ABOVE TREND, GUBRF (1W: +5.34% | 1M: +10.93%) ABOVE TREND, KRDMD (1W: +1.12% | 1M: +11.56%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: +0.06% | 1M: +11.78%) ABOVE TREND, PETKM (1W: -0.83% | 1M: -3.20%) BELOW TREND.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
PGSUS (1W: -8.95% | 1M: -9.04%) BELOW TREND, AEFES (1W: -9.54% | 1M: -4.22%) BELOW TREND, EKGYO (1W: -9.55% | 1M: -11.61%) BELOW TREND, SAHOL (1W: -9.64% | 1M: -12.28%) BELOW TREND, KCHOL (1W: -9.70% | 1M: -5.92%) BELOW TREND.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Up-momentum: ASELS (1W: +9.50% | 1M: +12.12%) ABOVE TREND. Down-momentum: TKFEN (1W: -14.02% | 1M: -8.08%) BELOW TREND, KONTR (1W: -12.59% | 1M: -10.53%) BELOW TREND, HEKTS (1W: -12.31% | 1M: -15.18%) BELOW TREND, ARCLK (1W: -10.85% | 1M: -2.72%) BELOW TREND.

SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banks are under pressure and broadly BELOW TREND on 1W losses; systemic names are weak. AKBNK (1W: -8.41% | 1M: -10.97%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -7.64% | 1M: -9.30%) BELOW TREND, ISCTR (1W: -8.84% | 1M: -5.34%) BELOW TREND, HALKB (1W: -1.49% | 1M: +13.81%) CORRECTION.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy Industry shows pockets of strength but mixed internals; defense and steel lead while autos and appliances lag. ASELS (1W: +9.50% | 1M: +12.12%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: +0.06% | 1M: +11.78%) ABOVE TREND, KRDMD (1W: +1.12% | 1M: +11.56%) ABOVE TREND, ARCLK (1W: -10.85% | 1M: -2.72%) BELOW TREND.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy is soft overall with selective rebounds; GUBRF and TRMET stand out vs. broad weakness. GUBRF (1W: +5.34% | 1M: +10.93%) ABOVE TREND, TRMET (1W: -1.88% | 1M: +12.75%) CORRECTION, TUPRS (1W: -0.97% | 1M: -8.17%) BELOW TREND.

PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Cautious selective long bias: trim cyclical exposure and avoid broad sector bets until breadth improves; overweight high-conviction names showing ABOVE TREND (ASELS, EREGL) and keep cash for volatility dislocations. Maintain strict stop discipline on BELOW TREND names and avoid averaging into structurally weak holdings.

NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
ASELS (1W: +9.50% | 1M: +12.12%) ABOVE TREND for momentum confirmation; SAHOL (1W: -9.64% | 1M: -12.28%) BELOW TREND for downside risk/catalyst flow; TKFEN (1W: -14.02% | 1M: -8.08%) BELOW TREND for tradeable overshoot; TUPRS (1W: -0.97% | 1M: -8.17%) BELOW TREND for energy-price sensitivity.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Heavy Industry for continued relative strength, Banking for macro sensitivity to FX and policy comments, Energy & Chemicals for commodity-driven reversals. Watch if any sector shifts from negative to positive breadth — that will drive re-risking.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 reaction to near-term support breach (failure to hold recent weekly lows would extend the drawdown) and reclaim of the 1M highs as a bullish trigger; monitor USD/TRY, CBRT commentary, Q4 earnings revisions, commodity prices and global risk tone as primary catalysts.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Neutral regime with normal volatility but zero sector breadth — market is fragile and participation is narrow. Look for selective opportunities in ABOVE TREND names (ASELS, EREGL, GUBRF) while keeping broad exposure light and stops tight given pervasive 1W weakness.
